question: How many total acres will have burned in U.S. wildfires in 2026, per NIFC's year-to-date total published on July 24, 2026?

factors.description: Empirical July10->24 national YTD increment 2016-2025 (mean 732k, median 768k, std ~360k) applied to 3.445M anchor gives central ~4.2M with wide right-skewed spread.
2026 running hot: 137% of 10yr average, PL4, 44 active large fires, western heat dome forecast -> tilt above median; quiet-year (2020/2023) increments downweighted.
Metric increases monotonically; already 3.578M as of July 11, so floor ~3.6M and lower bound 3.475 essentially unreachable.

summary: Discrete measurement of a monotonically increasing quantity: NIFC year-to-date acres burned, as displayed July 24, 2026. Anchored on the July 10 value of 3.445M (latest July 12 IMSR = 3.578M through July 11). I applied the empirical distribution of national July 10→24 YTD increments across 2016-2025 (mean ~732k, median ~768k, std ~360k acres; range 116k in quiet 2023 to 1.51M in extreme 2017). Because 2026 is running hot (137% of 10yr average, PL4, 44 large fires, western heat dome forecast), I downweighted the two quiet-season increments and applied a modest tilt, yielding a right-skewed distribution centered ~4.27M. Lower tail bounded near 3.6M (metric already at 3.578M and rising); upper tail reaches ~5.2M in a 2017/2022-style megafire-run scenario. P(<3.475)≈0, P(>5.525)≈0.
reasoning:
Forecast submitted. 

**Summary:** This is a measurement question on a monotonically increasing quantity — NIFC's year-to-date US wildfire acreage as displayed July 24, 2026.

**Method:** The research sub-agent retrieved exact NIFC IMSR values, giving me two key inputs:
- **Current state:** 3.445M acres (July 10 anchor), 3.578M as of July 11 — already rising fast in a hot season (137% of the 10-year average, PL4, 44 large fires, heat dome).
- **Empirical increment distribution:** Across 2016-2025, the July 10→24 increment averaged ~732k acres (median 768k, std ~360k), ranging from +116k (quiet 2023) to +1.51M (extreme 2017).

I ran a Monte Carlo bootstrapping those 10 empirical increments, downweighting the quiet years given 2026's active conditions, producing a right-skewed distribution centered at **~4.26M acres**:
- P10: 3.86M · P50: 4.26M · P90: 4.85M
- Lower tail floored near 3.6M (metric can't decrease meaningfully); upper tail to ~5.2M in a megafire-run scenario.

The premortem reviewer approved, independently arriving at ~4.20-4.27M and confirming all factors against primary NIFC sources.
